sanskrit:
यज्ञार्थात्कर्मणोऽन्यत्र लोकोऽयं कर्मबन्धनः।
तदर्थं कर्म कौन्तेय मुक्तसङ्गः समाचर।।
transliteration:
yajñārthāt karmaṇo ’nyatra loko ’yaṃ karma-bandhanaḥ |
tad-arthaṃ karma kaunteya mukta-saṅgaḥ samācara ||
literal_meaning: Work done for the sake of sacrifice (yajña) alone / otherwise / this world / is bound by action / for that purpose / action / O son of Kuntī / freed from attachment / perform perfectly.
contextual_meaning: Actions performed as an offering to something larger than the ego dissolve psychological bondage; actions performed for self-centred gain reinforce the mental chains of guilt, pride, and anxiety.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance between the need to act and the fear of entanglement is resolved by shifting the locus of reward from self to a transpersonal value.
trigger_scenario: You’re working late to meet a deadline. If the goal is ‘to impress the boss’ you feel drained; if the goal is ‘to serve the team’s wellbeing’ the same overtime feels energising and free.
anchor_text: Work as an offering, not for ego, and you stay free.
